steps.title: HighSpeed MACSQuant Tyto cartridge (Miltenyi Biotec,...

steps.title: Thaw frozen blood at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Thaw frozen blood at 37 °C for 3 min, then transfer to ice (see Figure 1 for pictures of steps A1–3). Figure

steps.title: Removing a blood clot sample from a serum-separating tube
steps.instruction: Removing a blood clot sample from a serum-separating tube. (A) Frozen blood clot. (B) Frozen blood clot is thawed at 37 °C for 3 min in the water bath. (C) Thawed blood clot. The gel layer is also thawed in this picture, and some leftover serum can be seen above this gel, indicating that this blood clot is sufficiently thawed. (D) Cleaning of the rim of the serum-separating tube using 70% ethanol. (E) The serum-separating tube is inverted inside a 50 mL conical tube. (F) Sample after initial centrifugation. The blood clot will move to be under the gel layer. (G) Removal of the serum-separating tube. The gel layer is left behind inside the serum-separating tube, leaving the blood clot inside the 50 mL conical tube. (H) Blood clot is ready for step A4 of the procedure.

steps.title: Clean the outside rim area of...
steps.instruction: Clean the outside rim area of the tube with Kimwipes and 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge the serum-separating blood tube containing...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the serum-separating blood tube containing the blood clot upside down inside a 50 mL conical tube at 2,000 rcf for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Put the loosened clot into a gentleMACS C tube
steps.instruction: Put the loosened clot into a gentleMACS C tube.

steps.title: Add 10 mL of ice-cold RBC...
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of ice-cold RBC lysis solution to rinse the 50 mL conical tube to obtain as much sample as possible using a serological pipette. Add everything to the C tube. Close the C tube tightly.

steps.title: Run the Multi_A_01 Miltenyi protocol on the gentleMACS Octo Dissociator
steps.instruction: Run the Multi_A_01 Miltenyi protocol on the gentleMACS Octo Dissociator.

steps.title: Vortex the tube vigorously for 3 s
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube vigorously for 3 s.

steps.title: Place the sample on a nutator...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a nutator at 4 °C for 5 min. Note: If using fresh samples, increase this to 10 min.

steps.title: Pipette out the lysis solution into...
steps.instruction: Pipette out the lysis solution into a new 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Rinse the C tube with 5...
steps.instruction: Rinse the C tube with 5 mL of lysis buffer and then add this to the 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,000 rcf for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,000 rcf for 5 min at 4 °C to pellet the leukocytes. Note: It is normal for the leukocyte pellet to be reddish.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of RBC lysis solution to the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of RBC lysis solution to the pellet.

steps.title: Vortex the tube vigorously for 3 s
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube vigorously for 3 s.

steps.title: Place the sample on a nutator...
steps.instruction: Place the sample on a nutator at 4 °C for 5 min. Note: If using fresh samples, increase this to 10 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,000 rcf for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,000 rcf for 5 min at 4 °C to pellet the leukocytes.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Proceed to additional protocols depending on your endpoint
steps.instruction: Proceed to additional protocols depending on your endpoint. a. Proceed to section B if flow sorting is necessary for bulk assays. b. Skip to section C if performing single-nuclei assay. c. Optional: Add a suitable volume of 90% fetal bovine serum and 10% DMSO to freeze desired aliquots of the leukocytes in cryovials for future use. Note: Cell count varies per individual. Aliquoting samples may not be feasible if flow sorting is necessary due to the potential low cell count per aliquot of the desired population. B. Flow cytometry of lymphocytes

steps.title: Make MACS buffer fresh (see Recipe 2)
steps.instruction: Make MACS buffer fresh (see Recipe 2). Scale up or down depending on the number of samples. Make 10 mL per sorted population. Note: One blood clot tube yields a sample of B cells and T cells for a total of two sorted populations.

steps.title: Add 2 mL of MACS buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of MACS buffer to the leukocyte pellet and gently pipette up and down 3 times.

steps.title: Put a 70 μm MACS SmartStrainer...
steps.instruction: Put a 70 μm MACS SmartStrainer on a 5 mL conical tube and prime the strainer with 500 μL of MACS buffer.

steps.title: Filter using a 70 μm MACS...
steps.instruction: Filter using a 70 μm MACS SmartStrainer in the conical tube.

steps.title: Rinse the filter with an additional...
steps.instruction: Rinse the filter with an additional 500 μL of MACS buffer. Note: The total volume of the conical tube should now be 3 mL.

steps.title: Take out 200 μL of cell...
steps.instruction: Take out 200 μL of cell suspension and place it in a microcentrifuge tube to use as unstained controls.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 rcf for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 rcf for 5 min.

steps.title: Pipette out the supernatant
steps.instruction: Pipette out the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of MACS buffer to the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of MACS buffer to the pellet. Flick the tube gently five times to resuspend.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of each antibody...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of each antibody so that the dilution is 1:

steps.title: Flick the tube gently five times...
steps.instruction: Flick the tube gently five times to mix and incubate at 4 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: During this incubation period, make compensation...
steps.instruction: During this incubation period, make compensation bead controls by adding one drop of compensation beads and 1 μL of antibody to a microcentrifuge tube. Make a control for each antibody of interest in separate tubes.

steps.title: Add 3 mL of MACS buffer to the stained cells
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL of MACS buffer to the stained cells.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 rcf for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 rcf for 5 min.

steps.title: Pipette out the supernatant and replace...
steps.instruction: Pipette out the supernatant and replace it with 1 mL of MACS buffer.

steps.title: Put a 30 μm MACS SmartStrainer...
steps.instruction: Put a 30 μm MACS SmartStrainer on a 5 mL Polyprene round-bottom tube and prime the strainer with 500 μL of MACS buffer.

steps.title: Filter the cell suspension using a 30 μm MACS SmartStrainer
steps.instruction: Filter the cell suspension using a 30 μm MACS SmartStrainer.

steps.title: Rinse the strainer with 500 μL of MACS buffer. Note
steps.instruction: Rinse the strainer with 500 μL of MACS buffer. Note: The total volume of the conical tube should now be 2 mL. The sample can be diluted further with additional MACS buffer if needed for flow sorting. This should be optimized for the flow cytometer device being used.

steps.title: Proceed to flow sorting. Note
steps.instruction: Proceed to flow sorting. Note: See Reports S1, S2, and S3 in the Supplementary information for gating strategies. C. Nuclei extraction from whole leukocytes or flow-sorted cells

steps.title: Add 1 mL of ice-cold lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of ice-cold lysis buffer to the cell pellet.

steps.title: Transfer the solution to a gentleMACS C tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the solution to a gentleMACS C tube.

steps.title: Rinse the conical tube containing the...
steps.instruction: Rinse the conical tube containing the leftover leukocyte pellet with 1 mL of ice-cold lysis buffer. Transfer the solution into the same C tube.

steps.title: Close the C tube tightly beyond...
steps.instruction: Close the C tube tightly beyond the first resistance and place it onto the gentleMACS dissociator.

steps.title: Place Octocoolers around the samples on the gentleMACS dissociator
steps.instruction: Place Octocoolers around the samples on the gentleMACS dissociator.

steps.title: Run the gentleMACS program 4C_Nuclei_1 on the gentleMACS dissociator
steps.instruction: Run the gentleMACS program 4C_Nuclei_1 on the gentleMACS dissociator. Note: If using fresh samples, run this protocol twice for complete lysis.

steps.title: After the run, detach the C...
steps.instruction: After the run, detach the C tube from the gentleMACS dissociator and immediately place it on ice.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 5 min...
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 5 min (incubate for 10 min if using fresh samples). Note: If flow cytometry was done , skip directly to section D after this step.

steps.title: During this incubation, attach a MACS...
steps.instruction: During this incubation, attach a MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) to a 15 mL conical tube. Prime the strainer with 500 μL of lysis buffer.

steps.title: Run the nuclei suspension through the...
steps.instruction: Run the nuclei suspension through the primed MACS SmartStrainer (70 μm) and collect the flowthrough in a 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Wash the MACS strainer with 2 mL of lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Wash the MACS strainer with 2 mL of lysis buffer. Discard the strainer.

steps.title: Centrifuge the nuclei suspension at 300...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nuclei suspension at 300 rcf for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: During the centrifugation step, attach a...
steps.instruction: During the centrifugation step, attach a MACS SmartStrainer (30 μm) to a 5 mL conical tube. Prime the strainer with 500 μL of resuspension buffer.

steps.title: Aspirate/pipette off the supernatant completely
steps.instruction: Aspirate/pipette off the supernatant completely.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet with 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet with 1 mL of resuspension buffer by pipetting up and down gently 10 times.

steps.title: Run the nuclei suspension through the...
steps.instruction: Run the nuclei suspension through the primed MACS SmartStrainer (30 μm) and collect the flowthrough in a 5 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of resuspension buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of resuspension buffer to the strainer to rinse out any remaining nuclei. D. Nuclei cleanup and concentration

steps.title: Centrifuge nucleus suspension at 300 rcf for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge nucleus suspension at 300 rcf for 5 min.

steps.title: Aspirate/pipette off the supernatant completely
steps.instruction: Aspirate/pipette off the supernatant completely.

steps.title: Resuspend the nucleus pellet in 450...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the nucleus pellet in 450 μL of nuclei separation buffer. Note: If the entire blood clot was used instead of flow sorting, increase this volume 5 times.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of anti-nucleus MicroBeads. Note
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of anti-nucleus MicroBeads. Note: If the entire blood clot was used instead of flow sorting, increase this volume 5 times.

steps.title: Mix well by gentle pipetting and...
steps.instruction: Mix well by gentle pipetting and incubate for 15 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: During this incubation period, calibrate the LS columns
steps.instruction: During this incubation period, calibrate the LS columns.

steps.title: Mount the QuadroMACS Separator Unit to the MACS Multistand
steps.instruction: Mount the QuadroMACS Separator Unit to the MACS Multistand.

steps.title: Place the LS column onto the separator unit
steps.instruction: Place the LS column onto the separator unit.

steps.title: Rinse the column with 3 mL...
steps.instruction: Rinse the column with 3 mL of nuclei separation buffer and allow the buffer to drip through the column. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Add 2 mL of nuclei separation...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of nuclei separation buffer to the nucleus suspension from step D

steps.title: Apply the nucleus suspension to the column
steps.instruction: Apply the nucleus suspension to the column. Apply directly to the center of the column reservoir. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Wait for the column to stop...
steps.instruction: Wait for the column to stop dripping before proceeding to the next step.

steps.title: Wash the column twice with 1...
steps.instruction: Wash the column twice with 1 mL of nuclei separation buffer. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Make sure to rinse the inner...
steps.instruction: Make sure to rinse the inner column wall with these wash steps.

steps.title: Remove the column from the separator...
steps.instruction: Remove the column from the separator and place it inside a 5 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Add 500 μL of nuclei separation...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of nuclei separation buffer onto the column and immediately flush out the magnetically labeled nuclei by firmly pushing the plunger into the column.

steps.title: Count and visualize nuclei prior to downstream assays
steps.instruction: Count and visualize nuclei prior to downstream assays.

description: Validation of protocol We successfully performed the entirety of this protocol and yielded intact nuclei from frozen blood clots that had been frozen for 5 months. Flow cytometry was done successfully using both the Sony MA900 Multi Application Cell Sorter (see Figure 2 and Report S1) and the Miltenyi MACSQuant Tyto Cell Sorter (see Report S2 and S3) for B and T cells from frozen blood clots, suggesting the leukocyte extraction protocol is gentle on the fragile cells and cell sorting is possible despite the cells being non-viable. The yield was higher when using the Tyto sorter, likely due to the constant agitation possible within the cartridge to prevent clumping of cells (see Table 1 ). Nuclei morphology suggests an intact nuclear envelope without severe blebbing, as indicated by Figure 1 . As a validation experiment, we performed bulk ATAC-seq on B and T cells obtained from sorted 5-month-old frozen blood clots. The OMNI ATAC seq library prep, outlined by Grandi et al. [18], was followed, starting from the tagmentation step through library prep. Barcoding primers used were AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTGATGAAATCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTAT index i5 + CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATTCGCCTTAGTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTG index i7 for CD19+ cells and AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACGTCGGACTTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTAT index i5 + CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATCTAGTACGGTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTG index i7 for CD3+ cells. As seen in Table 2 , the library prep for bulk ATAC-seq was successful and met quality score thresholds. The “% >= Q30 bases” metric indicates that both samples are greater than the 70% threshold for ATAQ-seq. The mean quality scores of 36.86 and 37.5 are indicative of a close to 1:10,000 probability of incorrect bases called, suggesting 99.99% accuracy. Figure 4 displays read depths of open chromatin regions from nuclei extracted from flow-sorted B and T cells. These peaks are indicative of the expected open chromatin signatures of these cell types. Together, this data suggests that isolat
